prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. In the third turning of the moon called “Q-three,” the great coffers of the Cloud Sages swelled by forty-and-two “ARR marks,” a coin unknown to man or beast, yet counted by bean-counters who wear no armor.  
2. Lo, the churn—no churn of butter, but of souls—didst shrink by a “hundred bips,” a measure so tiny it vanished like a whisper in the privy.  
3. A dragon named “Net Revenue Retention” belched flame at 110 %, scorching every prior scroll and forcing this humble monk to ask if numbers can now exceed their own skins.  
4. The high priests spake of “pipeline coverage” thrice-fold, yet I walked the battlements and saw nary a pipe, only glowing rectangles that ping when touched, as if the saints were restless in their reliquaries.
